content:
- Required gates are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after the verify-image security-floor boundary move.
- Updated the bug task file to `<status>completed</status>` and `<passes>true</passes>`, ticked the acceptance criteria, and ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`, which refreshed Ralph summaries and selected the next task.
- Next after quitting immediately: nothing further for this bug task itself; commit the staged task-finished work (including `.ralph` bookkeeping) and push, unless the commit/push already happened in this turn.

- Finished the bug task end to end: moved verify-image security floor checks to the artifact boundary, refreshed the MOLT module floors, passed `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, updated the task to completed/passes=true, ran `task_switch.sh`, committed, and pushed.
- Final commit: `54d89b4` on `master` (`task finished bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings: enforce verify image security floors at the artifact boundary`).
- Extra note: exploratory `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...` inside `cockroachdb_molt/molt` still reaches DB-backed verify packages that require Cockroach on 127.0.0.1:26257/26258; this is outside the repo's required `make test` gate, which passed.

- Read previous Ralph progress and refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` / `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.
- Verified the refreshed active-task summary contains a single candidate: story 21 task 07, a non-bug task with `<passes>false</passes>`.
- Read the task file directly and confirmed no `<blocked_by>` tag is present, so it is unblocked and becomes the mandatory next pick under the chooser precedence.
- Checked top-level repo structure to ground the project identity in the actual codebase before selecting.
- Next after quitting immediately: write `.ralph/current_task.txt` to `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest.md` and hand off execution to the implementation agent.

- Created `.ralph/current_task.txt` because it was missing.
- Wrote the selected next task path: `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest.md`.
- Selection reason: after refreshing Ralph summaries, there was exactly one active unblocked failing task and no unblocked failing bug tasks, so chooser precedence requires this task next.
- Next after quitting immediately: execute story 21 task 07 with the `tdd` skill and validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all stay green.

- Read saved progress, confirmed the selected task path from `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and opened the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions.
- Confirmed this turn must follow the task-file state machine: either create/advance a plan and quit, or execute only if the task already says `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: read the story task file, determine whether it is planning-only or execution, and append the decision back to the progress log immediately.

- Read the task file and confirmed it has no existing `<plan>` entry or execution marker, so this turn is planning-only and must stop after writing the plan.
- Inspected `.github/workflows/publish-images.yml`, workflow-specific `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md`, and story-21 history to ground the design in the current publish topology: Quay platform refs first, GHCR manifest fan-in later, and workflow verification required online.
- Found a live boundary drift: older story plans still reference Rust workflow-contract helpers that no longer exist in the repo, while workflow-local instructions now forbid creating local workflow test suites. The plan must explicitly resolve that by using one source-controlled workflow boundary plus hosted/API verification instead of reviving stale local YAML-test architecture blindly.
- Next: finish reading the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance, then write the new task plan file, add its path to the task markdown, and quit immediately.

- Created the planning artifact for story 21 task 07 at `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest_plans/2026-04-20-manual-image-retag-plan.md` and added its `<plan>` path to the task markdown.
- The plan is marked `NOW EXECUTE` because the interface is now concrete: add a separate manual `workflow_dispatch` promotion workflow, treat GHCR commit-SHA refs as the public release-promotion boundary, keep Quay as the upstream publish/security gate, and verify the flow through authenticated GitHub API dispatch instead of local workflow tests.
- Key design constraints captured in the plan: required `version` input plus boolean `set_latest`, source commit implied by the dispatched `master` head SHA, full three-image promotion, no rebuild/test reruns in the manual workflow, no swallowed registry/API errors, and immediate switch back to `TO BE VERIFIED` if hosted reality proves GHCR-only promotion or implicit source SHA is wrong.
- Next after quitting immediately: resume at Slice 0, re-check the latest hosted workflow state with `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl`, then implement and hosted-verify the manual release-tag workflow exactly as planned.

- Read saved progress, the current task markdown, and the execution plan; confirmed this turn is `NOW EXECUTE` and must follow hosted workflow verification instead of local workflow tests.
- Read `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md`, `publish-images.yml`, `README.md`, and the GitHub auth-wrapper skill; verified the live repo currently has only one active workflow (`publish-images`) and no existing manual promotion workflow online.
- Identified the clean workflow boundary to implement next: a separate `workflow_dispatch` GHCR-only promotion workflow that uses the already-published commit-SHA tags as source, installs only `skopeo`, avoids checkout/build/test steps entirely, and keeps the three-image target list in one place.
- Next if interrupted immediately: finish adding `.github/workflows/promote-image-tags.yml`, then push it so hosted dispatch verification can begin against the real GitHub Actions surface.

- Added `.github/workflows/promote-image-tags.yml` as a separate `workflow_dispatch`-only manual release workflow. It stays GHCR-only, has required `version` and boolean `set_latest` inputs, does explicit master-ref/input validation, logs into GHCR with `GITHUB_TOKEN`, and promotes the three existing GHCR commit-SHA image refs using `skopeo copy --all` without checkout/build/test steps.
- Pushed an intermediate verification commit `5daf0fc` (`Add manual GHCR image promotion workflow`) to `master` so the hosted workflow could be indexed and tested on real GitHub Actions.
- Verified online that GitHub now lists two active workflows (`publish-images` and `promote-image-tags`) and that the new push triggered `publish-images` run `24677591189` for commit `5daf0fca70c75f5927d6df365f08586ed207da49`.
- Current blocker: the upstream hosted `publish-images` run has completed all six image publish lanes successfully but `validate-long` is still in progress, so `publish-manifest` has not yet produced the GHCR commit-SHA tags needed for positive manual-promotion dispatch testing.
- Next if interrupted immediately: wait for run `24677591189` to finish, confirm GHCR commit-SHA tags for `5daf0fca70c75f5927d6df365f08586ed207da49` exist, then dispatch `promote-image-tags` twice through `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl` (`set_latest=false` then `true`) and inspect workflow runs plus resulting tags.

- Hosted red verification exposed a real design break: `promote-image-tags` run `24678268137` on commit `5daf0fca70c75f5927d6df365f08586ed207da49` failed in `Promote published GHCR images` with `Error parsing image name \"docker://ghcr.io/runner:5daf...\": ... name invalid`.
- The workflow log shows the live repository variables currently resolve to bare names `runner`, `setup-sql`, and `verify`, so both the new manual workflow and existing `publish-manifest` logic are deriving invalid GHCR refs instead of a valid owner/package path. This contradicts the execution design assumption that the GHCR commit-SHA source surface was already a settled boundary.
- Per the task state-machine instructions, switched the plan file back from `NOW EXECUTE` to `TO BE VERIFIED` and documented the hosted finding in the plan. Execution must stop now rather than forcing the wrong registry contract.
- Next if interrupted immediately: redesign the shared GHCR image-reference boundary first by reconciling README, `publish-images.yml`, `promote-image-tags.yml`, and live repo variable naming; only then resume execution and rerun hosted dispatch verification.

- Confirmed the active task still points to a plan ending in TO BE VERIFIED, so this turn is planning/design only unless the GHCR reference boundary can be made explicit.
- Read the task markdown and current plan; the blocker is the unresolved GHCR owner/package naming contract between README, workflows, and live repo variables after the hosted dispatch failure.
- Next: inspect README and workflow files plus live GitHub variable values, then either update the plan back to NOW EXECUTE with a concrete boundary or leave it TO BE VERIFIED and stop.

- Inspected publish-images.yml, promote-image-tags.yml, README.md, workflow-local AGENTS guidance, prior story-21 plans, and improve-code-boundaries smells relevant to config drift.
- Verified the real boundary bug: both workflows currently treat mutable repository variables as if they were GHCR-ready image coordinates, but README and prior story design expect canonical source-controlled repository names plus a separate GHCR owner namespace.
- Next: patch the task plan to make that boundary explicit, switch the plan to NOW EXECUTE, and stop immediately so the next turn can execute against a stable design.

- Updated the task-07 plan to resolve the GHCR boundary explicitly: canonical source-controlled repositories are `cockroach-migrate-runner`, `cockroach-migrate-setup-sql`, and `cockroach-migrate-verify`; Quay keeps `vars.QUAY_ORGANIZATION`; GHCR uses `${{ github.repository_owner }}`.
- Switched the plan from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE because README, prior story plans, and the hosted failure together are enough to prove the mutable per-image repo variables are the wrong boundary.
- Next if interrupted immediately: resume execution from Slice 0/1 only, fix `publish-images.yml` and `promote-image-tags.yml` together around one shared image catalog, then continue with hosted push/dispatch verification.

- Read the active task and execution plan; confirmed the plan ends in NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must implement and verify unless hosted reality forces TO BE VERIFIED again.
- Re-entering execution at Slice 0/1 to inspect the current workflow files, workflow-local guidance, and working tree around the GHCR image-reference boundary before patching anything.
- Next if interrupted immediately: compare publish/promote workflow image naming against README and current git state, then patch the shared image catalog boundary and continue with hosted verification.

- Inspected workflow-local guidance, current publish/promote workflow YAML, and README; the checked-in code still derives GHCR repositories from mutable repo variables even though README teaches source-controlled `cockroach-migrate-*` package names.
- Moving into Slice 0 by checking the live GitHub workflow inventory and repo identity before editing, so the boundary fix matches the current hosted surface rather than stale assumptions.
- Next if interrupted immediately: patch both workflows to own the canonical image catalog in source, then push and verify the hosted publish/promotion runs against the corrected GHCR refs.

- Verified Slice 0 against the live GitHub Actions API: the repo only has `publish-images` and `promote-image-tags` active, so the current task scope still matches hosted reality.
- Confirmed both checked-in workflows still leak `RUNNER_IMAGE_REPOSITORY` / `SETUP_SQL_IMAGE_REPOSITORY` / `VERIFY_IMAGE_REPOSITORY` into the GHCR path, which matches the hosted failure and makes Slice 1 the correct immediate fix.
- Next if interrupted immediately: patch the shared image catalog across publish/promote workflows, then push and wait for the hosted publish run that republishes the corrected GHCR commit-SHA tags.

- Patched the workflows to remove mutable per-image repository variables from the GHCR boundary.
- Added a source-controlled reusable workflow `.github/workflows/image-catalog.yml` that owns the canonical image catalog and now feeds both `publish-images.yml` and `promote-image-tags.yml`; updated README to describe version/latest as manual promotion of those same GHCR packages.
- Next if interrupted immediately: review the YAML carefully, then commit/push the boundary fix and inspect the hosted `publish-images` run for the new commit before dispatching manual promotion again.

- Reviewed the workflow refactor locally; both workflows now consume the reusable image catalog and the mutable GHCR repository variables are gone from the edited files.
- Preparing the hosted red/green loop now: check branch/diff sanity, then push this boundary fix so GitHub can validate the reusable workflow wiring and republish canonical GHCR commit-SHA tags.
- Next if interrupted immediately: commit the workflow boundary fix and watch the resulting `publish-images` run on GitHub for any syntax/runtime failures.
